---
title: "bbk: Accessing Central Bank Data in R"
subtitle: "One interface for 17 central banks"
author:
  - name: Maximilian Mücke
    affiliations:
      - name: LMU Munich
date: 2026-07-07
date-format: "[useR! 2026 · Warsaw ·] YYYY-MM-DD"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    embed-resources: true
    transition: none
    slide-number: "c/t"
    progress: false
    footer: "useR! 2026"
    incremental: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: arrow
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 4.3
    fig-align: center
    fig-dpi: 150
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: true
    message: false
    warning: false
    cache: true
    dev: png
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
#| cache: false
library(bbk)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(data.table)

# Okabe-Ito: color-blind safe
ok = c(ECB = "#0072B2", BoE = "#D55E00", Riksbank = "#009E73")

# refined house style (matches the package README), sized for slides
theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#073b6c"),
      plot.caption = element_text(
        hjust = 0,
        vjust = 0,
        size = 13,
        color = "#5b6470",
        margin = margin(10, 0, 0, 0)
      ),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.2),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.title = element_blank()
    )
)
```

## Central bank data is everywhere {.smaller}

Policy rates, FX, yields, inflation — the backbone of economic research and
financial modelling.

. . .

But **every institution speaks its own dialect:**

| Institution | API style | Identifiers |
|---|---|---|
| ECB · Bundesbank · BIS · Norges Bank | SDMX | dotted keys |
| Czech NB · NBP · Riksbank · Banco de España | JSON REST | series codes |
| Bank of Canada | Valet JSON | series codes |
| Banco Central do Brasil | OData / Olinda | numeric codes |
| Banco de México | token REST | series tokens |
| Bank of Japan | stat-search JSON | db + series code |
| Bank of England | custom CSV | series codes |
| Banque de France | webstat CSV | dataset keys |
| Banco de Portugal | BPstat JSON | series ids |
| Österreichische Nationalbank | custom XML | hierarchy + key |
| Swiss National Bank | custom CSV / JSON | table ids |

Different base URLs, auth, formats, pagination → **access is painful.**

## `bbk`: one interface for all of them {.smaller}

17 central banks and growing, the **same verbs** everywhere:

```r
ecb_data(flow, key)      # fetch a time series
ecb_metadata("dataflow") # browse the catalog
ecb_dimension(id)        # explore a series' dimensions
```

Every call returns the same tidy `data.table` — swap the `ecb_` prefix for any
bank below:

```{r}
#| label: one-interface
#| R.options: !expr list(width = 100)
ecb_data("FM", "B.U2.EUR.4F.KR.DFR.LEV", last_n = 4)[, .(date, freq, title, value)]
```

```{=html}
<div class="banks">
<span>BCB</span><span>BdE</span><span>Banxico</span><span>BdP</span><span>BIS</span><span>BoC</span>
<span>BoE</span><span>BoJ</span><span>BdF</span><span>CNB</span><span>BBk</span><span>ECB</span>
<span>NBP</span><span>NoB</span><span>SRb</span><span>SNB</span><span>OeNB</span><span>+…</span>
</div>
```

## Go from identifier to plot in seconds {.smaller}

```{r}
#| label: yield
#| out-width: "100%"
#| fig-alt: "Line chart of the German 10-year government bond yield from 2015
#|   to 2026, rising from below zero around 2020 to roughly 2.5 percent."
yield = bbk_data(
  flow = "BBSIS", key = "D.I.ZAR.ZI.EUR.S1311.B.A604.R10XX.R.A.A._Z._Z.A",
  start_period = "2015-01-01"
)

ggplot(yield, aes(date, value)) +
  geom_line(color = "#1b2a3a", linewidth = 0.7) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "German 10-year government bond yield",
    caption = sprintf("Source: Deutsche Bundesbank (BBSIS), %s.", format(max(yield$date), "%B %Y"))
  )
```

## Don't know the code? Search for it {.smaller}

Query the catalog with `*_metadata()`, then filter by plain-language name:

```{=html}
<div class="discover-flow">
  <span class="stage">search "exchange rate"</span>
  <span class="arrow">→</span>
  <span class="stage hit">BBEX3</span>
  <span class="arrow">→</span>
  <span class="stage go">bbk_data()</span>
  <span class="arrow">→</span>
  <span class="stage chart">tidy data.table</span>
</div>
```

```{r}
#| label: discover
flows = bbk_metadata("dataflow")
flows[name %ilike% "exchange rate", .(id, name = substr(name, 1, 33))]

bbk_data("BBEX3", "D.USD.EUR.BB.AC.000", last_n = 4)[, .(date, key, freq, value)]
```

## Same shape, every bank {.smaller}

Three central banks, three near-identical calls, one tidy `data.table` each:

```{r}
#| label: policy-rates
#| output-location: column
#| out-width: "100%"
#| fig-width: 9
#| fig-height: 5.5
#| fig-alt: "Step chart comparing policy rates of the ECB, Bank of England and
#|   Sveriges Riksbank from 2015 to 2026: all near zero until 2022, then a sharp
#|   rise, with the Bank of England highest around 4-5 percent."
from = "2015-01-01"
ecb = ecb_data(
  flow = "FM", key = "B.U2.EUR.4F.KR.DFR.LEV",
  start_period = from
)
boe = boe_data("IUDBEDR", start_date = from)
srb = srb_data("SECBREPOEFF", start_date = from)

# each *_data() is a tidy data.table -> stack them
rates = rbindlist(
  list(ECB = ecb, BoE = boe, Riksbank = srb),
  idcol = "bank",
  fill = TRUE
)

ggplot(rates, aes(date, value, color = bank)) +
  geom_step(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = ok) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Central bank policy rates",
    caption = sprintf(
      "Source: ECB, Bank of England, Sveriges Riksbank, %s.",
      format(max(rates$date), "%B %Y")
    )
  )
```

## Take it home {.smaller}

::: {.lead}
One interface · 17 central banks · tidy output · reproducible.
:::

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="62%"}
```r
install.packages("bbk")
```

```{=html}
<div class="links">
<a href="https://cran.r-project.org/package=bbk"><svg class="ic" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4"/><polyline points="7 10 12 15 17 10"/><line x1="12" y1="15" x2="12" y2="3"/></svg><span><strong>CRAN</strong>: <code>bbk</code></span></a>
<a href="https://m-muecke.github.io/bbk/"><svg class="ic" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M2 3h6a4 4 0 0 1 4 4v14a3 3 0 0 0-3-3H2z"/><path d="M22 3h-6a4 4 0 0 0-4 4v14a3 3 0 0 1 3-3h7z"/></svg><span><strong>Docs</strong>: m-muecke.github.io/bbk</span></a>
<a href="https://github.com/m-muecke/bbk"><svg class="ic" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M9 19c-5 1.5-5-2.5-7-3m14 6v-3.87a3.37 3.37 0 0 0-.94-2.61c3.14-.35 6.44-1.54 6.44-7A5.44 5.44 0 0 0 20 4.77 5.07 5.07 0 0 0 19.91 1S18.73.65 16 2.48a13.38 13.38 0 0 0-7 0C6.27.65 5.09 1 5.09 1A5.07 5.07 0 0 0 5 4.77a5.44 5.44 0 0 0-1.5 3.78c0 5.42 3.3 6.61 6.44 7A3.37 3.37 0 0 0 9 18.13V22"/></svg><span><strong>Source</strong>: github.com/m-muecke/bbk</span></a>
</div>
```

*Want another central bank? Open an issue.*
:::

::: {.column width="38%"}
::: {.qr}
![](qr-bbk.png){width="62%" fig-alt="QR code linking to the bbk documentation website at m-muecke.github.io/bbk"}

Scan for the docs
:::
:::
::::
